query: mutation($track: TrackInput!) { updateCompleteTrack(track: $track) { id slug challenges { id slug title } } }

track.title: Aura SRE Platform — Intelligent Infrastructure Discovery
track.description: Deploy Aura into a live AWS environment running the Bella Vista restaurant app. Watch it discover infrastructure, build a knowledge base, and use 5 specialized AI agents to investigate, audit, and optimize.
track.teaser: AI-powered SRE platform that discovers AWS infrastructure and builds a semantic knowledge base

challenges.title: Welcome to Bella Vista
challenges.assignment:
## Welcome to Bella Vista

You just joined the platform team at **Bella Vista**, a growing restaurant chain running on AWS. Your manager says:

> *Here's your AWS Console access. The app is running. You're on-call starting Monday. Good luck.*

### Explore the Environment

Switch to the **AWS Console** tab. Try to answer:

1. What services are running in ECS?
2. What database backs the application?
3. Are any CloudWatch alarms firing?
4. How do orders flow through the system?

Notice how long it takes to find answers by clicking through the console.

In the next challenge, you'll see how Aura answers all of these in minutes.

> **Click Check when you're ready to continue.**

challenges.title: Discover Everything
challenges.assignment:
## Discover Everything

Aura is running on ECS in this environment. Let's trigger a full discovery.

First, find the ALB and check the orchestrator:

```
ALB_DNS=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?LoadBalancerName==`bella-vista-alb`].DNSName' --output text --region us-east-1)
echo "ALB: $ALB_DNS"
curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3030/health
```

Now trigger discovery:

```
curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3030/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Discover my AWS environment in us-east-1. Catalog everything."}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

This takes 2-3 minutes. The orchestrator dispatches parallel workers that call AWS APIs, parse resources, generate embeddings, and store in Qdrant.

**No raw AWS data passes through any LLM context.**

> **Click Check when discovery completes.**

challenges.title: Query Your Knowledge Base
challenges.assignment:
## Query Your Knowledge Base

The KB is populated. Ask Aura about the environment.

```
ALB_DNS=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?LoadBalancerName==`bella-vista-alb`].DNSName' --output text --region us-east-1)
```

**What ECS services exist?**
```
curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3030/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"What ECS services are running? List them with configurations."}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

**What depends on the database?**
```
curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3030/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"What depends on the Bella Vista database? Trace the dependency chain."}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

**Any security concerns?**
```
curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3030/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Are there security concerns? Check for permissive security groups and public S3 buckets."}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

Try your own questions!

> **Click Check when done exploring.**

challenges.title: Incident Response
challenges.assignment:
## Incident Response (Choose Your Own Adventure)

A CloudWatch alarm is firing. Let's triage with the incident response agent (port 3031).

```
ALB_DNS=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?LoadBalancerName==`bella-vista-alb`].DNSName' --output text --region us-east-1)

curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3031/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"The CloudWatch alarm bella-vista-5xx-high is firing. Triage: What is broken? What is the blast radius? What changed recently? What should we do?"}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

**Dig deeper:**
```
curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3031/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"If the Bella Vista database goes down completely, what is the full blast radius?"}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

> **Click Check when done.**

challenges.title: Change Audit
challenges.assignment:
## Change Audit (Choose Your Own Adventure)

Someone made a risky change. Let's find it using the change audit agent (port 3032).

```
ALB_DNS=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?LoadBalancerName==`bella-vista-alb`].DNSName' --output text --region us-east-1)

curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3032/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Scan CloudTrail for recent infrastructure changes. Risk-rate each change and flag anything concerning."}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

**Investigate the risky change:**
```
curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3032/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"The security group bella-vista-legacy-ssh-sg has SSH open to the world. What resources use it? What is the exposure?"}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

> **Click Check when done.**

challenges.title: Post-Mortem Construction
challenges.assignment:
## Post-Mortem Construction (Choose Your Own Adventure)

Construct a blameless post-mortem using the post-mortem agent (port 3033).

```
ALB_DNS=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?LoadBalancerName==`bella-vista-alb`].DNSName' --output text --region us-east-1)

curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3033/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Construct a blameless post-mortem for the Bella Vista checkout incident. The alarm bella-vista-5xx-high was firing. Reconstruct the timeline, identify contributing factors, assess blast radius, and recommend action items."}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

Look for: blameless language, timeline, contributing factors, action items, blast radius.

> **Click Check when done.**

challenges.title: Capacity Planning
challenges.assignment:
## Capacity Planning (Choose Your Own Adventure)

Find waste and resilience gaps using the capacity planning agent (port 3034).

```
ALB_DNS=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?LoadBalancerName==`bella-vista-alb`].DNSName' --output text --region us-east-1)

curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3034/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Run a full capacity planning review. Check for: underutilized resources, cost waste, resilience gaps, over-provisioned services, and quota limits."}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

Aura should find: single-AZ RDS, over-provisioned Lambda (3GB), stopped EC2 instances, untagged resources.

> **Click Check when done.**

challenges.title: Build Your Own Agent
challenges.assignment:
## Build Your Own Agent (Choose Your Own Adventure)

Create a custom agent config. Pick a persona: Security Auditor, Onboarding Guide, Cost Reporter, or your own.

Create the config:

```
cat > /tmp/my-agent.toml << 'EOF'
[llm]
provider = "bedrock"
model = "us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-20250514-v1:0"
region = "us-east-1"

[agent]
name = "my-custom-agent"
temperature = 0.3
turn_depth = 10
max_tokens = 8192
system_prompt = """
You are a Security Auditor for the Bella Vista platform.
Search the Qdrant KB for security risks:
- Permissive security groups (0.0.0.0/0)
- Public S3 buckets
- Excessive IAM permissions
- Missing tags
- Single points of failure
Present findings as a risk-rated table.
"""

[mcp.servers.qdrant]
transport = "http_streamable"
url = "http://localhost:8000/mcp"

[mcp.servers.aws_api]
transport = "http_streamable"
url = "http://localhost:8091/mcp"
EOF

cat /tmp/my-agent.toml
```

This demonstrates the config-only approach. No code. No deployment pipeline. Just a TOML file.

> **Click Check when done.**

challenges.title: AI-Generated Agent
challenges.assignment:
## AI-Generated Agent (Choose Your Own Adventure)

Ask Aura to design a new agent based on what it discovered.

```
ALB_DNS=$(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?LoadBalancerName==`bella-vista-alb`].DNSName' --output text --region us-east-1)

curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3030/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Generate a complete Aura TOML config for a Bella Vista Reliability Agent that monitors the critical order flow path, knows the risks you found, and can answer health questions. Output ONLY the TOML file."}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

The generated config references actual resource names from the KB.

**Try others:**
```
curl -s http://$ALB_DNS:3030/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Generate an Aura TOML config for a Bella Vista Runbook Agent that serves as the on-call runbook. Output ONLY the TOML."}]}' \
  | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content'
```

> **Click Check when done.**

challenges.title: Review & Next Steps
challenges.assignment:
## What You Accomplished

In under 30 minutes, Aura:

- Discovered and cataloged every resource in the Bella Vista environment
- Built a semantic knowledge base with cross-resource relationships
- Answered natural language questions about unfamiliar infrastructure
- Triaged incidents with blast radius analysis
- Audited changes and found SSH open to the world
- Constructed blameless post-mortems
- Found single-AZ database, over-provisioned Lambda, and cost waste
- Built custom agents from TOML configs
- Used AI to generate purpose-built agents from discovered knowledge

**This was real AWS infrastructure, not a mock.**

### Deploy in Your Environment

All configs are open source in the aura-examples repo.

> **Click Check to finish.**
